refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry logs and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or mitigate link failures to maintain availability.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and alerting system.
DP3: Integrate link recovery protocols and fallback modes.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with recovery mechanisms
Failover logic dependent on network link status
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically supports the FR with manageable coupling between monitoring and failover functions, enabling modular design.

assumptions_risks: Risk of increased complexity leading to potential failure modes.
Assumes failover mechanisms can operate within required latency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding explicit monitoring and recovery DPs if failover alone does not meet availability targets.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation link hardware and software including redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends link status and health data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections managed within Teleoperation module.

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion due to system-wide impact but low containment as link management is not centralized here.
Vehicle module interfaces with network but does not contain link availability logic.
Primary containment of link availability logic and failover mechanisms.
Receives alerts but does not control link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability logic within Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Integration complexity with other modules requiring link status data.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate redundancy within Teleoperation module itself to mitigate single point of failure.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR and supports measurable availability targets.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion of link status data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module effectively contains the core link availability logic, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and failover event logs is feasible within Teleoperation module boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for link availability to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding explicit monitoring and recovery sub-FRs and corresponding DPs in next decomposition level.
Assess Teleoperation module redundancy to reduce single point of failure risk.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, adding explicit sub-FRs for monitoring and recovery will improve clarity and traceability. Also, refining DP selection to include monitoring and recovery will reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or mitigate link failures to maintain availability.

revise.why: To explicitly capture monitoring and recovery functions and select corresponding DPs for comprehensive coverage.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
open_risks: Single point of failure in Teleoperation module.
Complexity of failover mechanisms impacting reliability.
evidence_needed: Failover event records and recovery time measurements.
